===Play-offs=== {{main|Super League IV#Play-offs}} Wigan's first round opponents in the Super League play-offs were Castleford Tigers. Playing for the first time at their new home ground, the [[JJB Stadium]], Wigan lost the match 10–14.{{cite web |title=Sport: Rugby League – Wigan crash out of play-offs |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/sport/rugby_league/451874.stm |website=BBC News |access-date=17 February 2025 |date=19 September 1999}} The defeat meant that Wigan finished the season without winning a trophy, the first time this had occurred since the [[1983–84 Rugby Football League season|1983–84 season]].{{cite web |title=Goodway forced out as Wigan ring the changes |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/goodway-forced-out-as-wigan-ring-the-changes-741416.html |website=The Independent |access-date=22 November 2024 |date=13 November 1999}} {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;" !Date !Round !Opponent !H/A !Result !Scorers !Att. |- style="background:#ffdddd |19 September 1999 |Elimination Play-off |{{Leagueicon|Castleford|16}} [[Castleford Tigers]] |H |10–14 | | |} ==Cup Run== {{main|1999 Challenge Cup}} {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;" !Date !Round !Opponent !H/A !Result !Scorers !Att. |- style="background:#ffdddd |14 February 1999 |Fourth Round |{{Leagueicon|Leeds|16}} [[Leeds Rhinos]] |A |18–28 | | |} Source:https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/competitions/challenge-cup/results/1999-00 {{Dead link|date=February 2022}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Wigan Warriors}} {{1999 in rugby league}} [[Category:Wigan Warriors seasons]] [[Category:1999 Super League season by club|Wigan Warriors]]
